title: Rich Canadian Beef Poutine
description: A rich and comforting Canadian dish made with tender beef, crispy fries, cheese curds, and a sweet and savory gravy.
instructions: Cut the french fries into 1/2-inch sticks and cook according to package instructions.
In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium-high heat.
Add the beef strips to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked through.
Transfer the cooked beef to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter and sprinkle with 2 tablespoons of all-purpose flour.
Whisk the mixture into a smooth paste and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Gradually whisk in the beef broth and gravy mix, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Add the sugar, salt, and pepper to the gravy and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until thickened.
Meanwhile, melt the cheese curds in the oven according to package instructions.
Assemble the poutine by placing a portion of cooked french fries in the bottom of a bowl, followed by a portion of melted cheese curds, and finally topped with a portion of beef strips and gravy.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
